label: Cautiously hopeful
summary: The war ends on paper. The U.S.-Iran memorandum to halt more than 100 days of fighting took effect as negotiating teams gathered in Geneva on Friday to formalize the deal that reopens the Strait of Hormuz without tolls, lifts the U.S. naval blockade and opens a 60-day window for nuclear talks; Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei said he had authorized the accord 'despite reservations,' and oil slid to its lowest since early March - U.S. crude near $75.83 - even as analysts warned it will take weeks to clear the tanker backlog. Markets exhaled: the Nasdaq jumped 1.91% and the S&P 500 1.08% as rate-hike fears eased and chipmakers rallied. Europe added to the steadier mood, with EU leaders opening the first cluster of Ukraine's accession talks and unanimously extending sanctions on Russia for another year. But the relief was uneven. Israeli forces and Hezbollah traded fresh fire in southern Lebanon despite the deal, with Washington pressing for 'a complete ceasefire on all fronts'; Russia kept striking Kyiv and Sumy overnight. Africa carried the day's heaviest ledger - more than 1,000 civilians killed in recent RSF drone strikes across Sudan, and a WHO-declared Bundibugyo Ebola emergency in DR Congo and Uganda now at 837 confirmed cases and 196 deaths. Against that, brighter notes: a landmark Lancet evaluation confirmed the RTS,S malaria vaccine has averted roughly one in eight child deaths in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi, and the Obama Presidential Center opened in Chicago's Jackson Park on Juneteenth. A signed peace and a market rebound lift the mood off the floor, but war's embers in Lebanon and Ukraine and crisis in Africa hold it at the hopeful edge of the middle.

drivers.headline: The U.S.-Iran memorandum to end the war takes effect as negotiating teams gather in Geneva on Friday to sign it, reopening the Strait of Hormuz without tolls, lifting the U.S. naval blockade and opening a 60-day window for nuclear talks

Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei says he authorized the memorandum of understanding with the U.S. 'despite reservations,' after assurances from President Pezeshkian that Iran's rights and the 'Resistance Front' would be safeguarded

Oil prices slide to their lowest since early March as the U.S.-Iran deal eases supply fears - U.S. crude down about 1.25% to $75.83 and Brent down 1.4% to $78.41 - though analysts warn it could take weeks to clear the Strait of Hormuz tanker backlog

Wall Street rebounds as rate-hike fears ease and chipmakers rally: the Nasdaq rises 1.91% to 26,517.93, the S&P 500 gains 1.08% to 7,500.58 and the Dow edges up 0.14%, with the U.S. ending its Iran oil blockade
At a high-stakes summit, EU leaders open the first cluster of Ukraine's accession negotiations - a historic milestone - and unanimously agree to extend economic sanctions on Russia for another year

Israeli forces and Hezbollah trade fresh fire in southern Lebanon despite the U.S.-Iran deal, with rockets fired at Israeli troops and Washington pressing for 'a complete ceasefire on all fronts, including Lebanon'

Russia keeps striking Ukraine: a series of explosions is heard in Kyiv and a man is killed by a drone attack on Sumy, as Ukraine reports Russian combat losses surpassing 1.38 million since the full-scale invasion
More than 1,000 civilians have been killed in recent RSF drone strikes across Sudan, with the deadliest hitting El Obeid in North Kordofan, as the war's toll on non-combatants sharply escalates

The Bundibugyo-virus Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda - a WHO-declared public health emergency of international concern - reaches 837 confirmed cases and 196 deaths across Ituri, North Kivu and South Kivu, with sustained transmission

A landmark Lancet evaluation confirms the RTS,S malaria vaccine has averted roughly one in eight child deaths over four years among eligible children in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi, pointing to high impact in wider rollout

The Obama Presidential Center opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on Juneteenth, a free community campus with a museum, library branch, gardens and athletic facilities on the city's South Side

Science keeps its steady pace: scientists report a single blood test able to detect around 50 cancer types before symptoms appear, an OpenAI reasoning model outperforms physicians on emergency-department diagnoses, and NASA tests a next-generation autonomous space computer chip

sources_consulted: Wikipedia (2026 Iran war ceasefire / 2026 Lebanon war / 2026 Central Africa Ebola epidemic / 2026 European heatwaves)
